question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Michigan and Oklahoma have similar populations.
options.B: The population gap between Michigan and Oklahoma might exceed that between Texas and Wisconsin.
options.C: The population differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.D: All the highlighted states have almost the same population levels.

explanation: Michigan (38.4M) and Oklahoma (49.3M) have a 10.9M population difference, while Texas (9.7M) and Wisconsin (10.3M) have only a 0.6M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Michigan and Oklahoma into the same color bin (30M+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Texas and Wisconsin appear more different due to different color bins.
